Name: Vermilion Flower
Story: You stumble upon a patch bright flowers in the sand.%A vermilion flower lurched over the jagged sand. It cannot outgrow its besieging captor; it can only hope to stretch skyward.%You capture the flower in a jar and seal the lid tightly.

Name: Amber Fossil
Story: The wind is strong here. You hear a haunting whistle around a cliff. Your landing party is unsettled.%You discover a cave opening is the source of the noise. The inside is darker than black.%The cave hides a mosquito trapped in an amber fossil, sealed in its golden tomb since before the age of names, for time does not release what it has chosen to keep.%The amber is smooth, you slip it in your pocket.

Name: Aureolin Ring
Story: You spot a glint in the moonlight on the beach.%A ring of aureolin gold, beautiful and warm as a dying sun, though every scholar who has written of it agrees on one thing: those who wear it near the onyx fog do not come back the same, if they come back at all.%The ring fits perfectly on your finger.

Name: Viridian Frog
Story: A strange guttural noise echos across the island.%You track it down to a freshwater pond. A group of frogs hiding under black algae. One frog stands out from the rest.%Its incandescent vertebrae segregates the viridian frog from any possible kin. What we deem beautiful quietly isolates the frog from its own home.%Its new home is in a glass case in your quarters.

Name: Cerulean Crystal
Story: A cerulean crystal humming with forgotten starlight, its facets etched by no mortal hand, said to whisper the names of lost oceans to those who dare hold it in moonless dark.

Name: Amethyst Statue
Story: Do not gaze into the eyes of the amethyst statue. There is no point of return from there.

Name: Onyx Fog
Story: Heed the warnings of the aureolin ring, for the onyx fog is hungry. Prowl through the nightfall and the haze will not hesitate to consume the light.

Name: Ivory Bones
Story: Ivory bones worn smooth by centuries of reverent hands, each one carved with sigils that glow faintly when the night grows too quiet and something old begins to listen.
